engineGuide.h2: Mercedes Benz GLC Class Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mercedes Benz GLC Class across all generations (X253, C253, X254), with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.1L Diesel
entries.history: The 2.1-litre OM651 powered early GLC 220d and 250d models, offering strong torque and efficiency. It became known for EGR and injector issues as mileages climbed.
commonFailures: Injector carbon clogging due to Bosch piezo system design
Swirl flap actuator failure (intake port shutoff)
Timing chain stretch on higher-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM651 Mercedes Benz GLC Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The OM654 launched in the 2019 GLC facelift as a cleaner, more efficient modular diesel. It features stepped bowl combustion and reduced friction, but introduced new emissions-related failure points.
compatibleVariants: GLC 220d, GLC 220d 4MATIC, GLC 300d, GLC 300d 4MATIC, GLC 300de, GLC 400d 4MATIC (OM656 variant for 400d)
commonFailures: EGR cooler cracking (A6540940101) due to thermal stress - dominant failure driver
AdBlue injector nozzle clogging (A0010780108) requiring SCR recalibration
NOx sensor failure causing emissions compliance faults
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM654 Mercedes Benz GLC Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel V6
entries.history: The OM642 3.0-litre V6 diesel powered the GLC 350d, delivering refined V6 performance. While smooth and powerful, its complex design created expensive repair challenges for owners.
commonFailures: Oil cooler seal leak within the engine "V" - labour-intensive repair
Intake port shutoff motor failure affecting EGR operation
Swirl flap linkage seizure
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM642 Mercedes Benz GLC Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The M274 2.0-litre turbocharged four-cylinder powered the majority of petrol GLC models through 2019. A member of Mercedes' modular engine family, it balanced performance with efficiency but gained a reputation for timing component failures.
compatibleVariants: GLC 200, GLC 200 4MATIC, GLC 250, GLC 250 4MATIC, GLC 300, GLC 300 4MATIC, GLC 350e
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing cold-start rattle (A2740500409) - most searched replacement driver
Camshaft adjuster/signal plate failure leading to poor starting
High-pressure fuel pump failure (shared with some BMW applications)
entries.cta: Get quotes for M274 Mercedes Benz GLC Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol (Mild Hybrid / EQ Boost)
entries.history: The M264 replaced the M274 in the 2019 facelift, introducing EQ Boost mild hybrid technology with an integrated starter-generator (BSG) and 48V electrical architecture. Complexity increased replacement costs significantly.
commonFailures: 48V belt-driven starter-generator (BSG) failure - A2641500060
Integrated exhaust manifold causing premature turbo wear
HPFP camshaft lobe wear on higher-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for M264 Mercedes Benz GLC Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol V6 Biturbo
entries.history: The M276 3.0-litre V6 biturbo powered the GLC 43 AMG, delivering 367-390hp. This sporty unit offered V6 refinement and strong mid-range punch, becoming the performance entry point below the V8 flagship.
commonFailures: Camshaft adjuster wear affecting timing accuracy
Oil leaks from camshaft solenoid seals
High-pressure fuel pump failures on tuned examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for M276 Mercedes Benz GLC Class engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol V8 Biturbo
entries.history: The hand-built M177 V8 biturbo powered the range-topping GLC 63 and 63 S AMG. Derived from the AMG GT's engine, it delivered 476-510hp through a sophisticated hot-vee design with twin-scroll turbos.
compatibleVariants: GLC 63 4MATIC+, GLC 63 S 4MATIC+, GLC 63 S 4MATIC+ Coupe
commonFailures: Cylinder bore scoring on higher-mileage examples
Oil consumption from piston ring wear
Turbocharger failure due to oil starvation - high replacement cost
entries.cta: Get quotes for M177 Mercedes Benz GLC Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol Plug-in Hybrid
entries.history: The GLC 350e combined the M274 petrol engine with an electric motor and 13.5kWh battery, offering up to 30 miles of electric range. Complex drivetrain integration created specialist-only repair requirements.
commonFailures: Hybrid battery degradation requiring module replacement
Electric motor bearing failure in drive unit
Charging system inverter faults
entries.cta: Get quotes for M274 Hybrid Mercedes Benz GLC Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol Plug-in Hybrid (X254)
entries.history: The second-generation GLC 300e uses the M254 engine with an upgraded plug-in hybrid system, delivering over 300hp and significantly extended electric range. The PHEV system adds complexity over the standard mild-hybrid M254.
commonFailures: Electric motor drive unit failure
High-voltage battery cell balancing issues
Hybrid coolant pump failures
entries.cta: Get quotes for M254 PHEV Mercedes Benz GLC Class engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mercedes Benz GLC Class Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mercedes GLC Class (X253, C253, X254) is a refined premium SUV, but its OM654 diesel and M274/M264 petrol engines have well-documented weak points that drive replacement demand. Timing chain stretch on earlier four-cylinders, EGR cooler cracking on the OM654, and camshaft adjuster failure on the V6 M276 are the most searched failure patterns. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the costs and decision points for the GLC's most common engine failures.

problems.group: Mercedes GLC OM654 EGR Cooler Cracking
problems.h4: Mercedes GLC OM654 EGR Cooler Cracking - The Most Searched Diesel Failure
problems.rootCause: The original EGR cooler (part A6540940101) suffers thermal stress fractures, allowing coolant to enter the exhaust system, triggering low coolant warnings, misfires, and eventually engine overheating.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace failed EGR cooler with revised OEM unit, drain and refill coolant, clear fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: 3-5 years (revised cooler more durable but same design)

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt engine with upgraded cooler
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of bench-tested OM654 rebuilt engine with reinforced EGR cooler upgrade (brazed A6540940001), new gaskets, timing components

problems.recommendation: If caught early with no overheating damage, replace just the EGR cooler. However, many cases present after coolant loss has caused cylinder head damage. On a GLC worth £18,000-£25,000, the full engine replacement with upgraded cooler is the safer long-term investment. Specialist pricing beats dealer by 30-40%.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM654 prices for your Mercedes GLC 220d or 300d

problems.group: Mercedes GLC M274/M264 Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes GLC M274/M264 Timing Chain Failure - The Cold Start Rattle
problems.affectedModels: GLC 200, GLC 250, GLC 300 (2015-2022, M274 and M264 engines)
problems.rootCause: The timing chain tensioner (A2740500409) loses hydraulic pressure during oil drain-back, causing chain slack on cold starts. Over time, this stretches the chain and damages guides and camshaft adjusters .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain tensioner only - valid if caught immediately when rattle first appears before chain stretch
repairOptions.longevity: Effective only if addressed within first 500 miles of noise

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides, sprockets. Labour intensive (8-10 hours).
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles with quality aftermarket or OEM components

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt engine with updated timing components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit bench-tested M274/M264 rebuilt engine with revised tensioner (A2740500912 recall-compliant) and upgraded chain guides

problems.recommendation: If you hear the characteristic 2-3 second rattle only on cold start with no fault codes, replace the tensioner immediately - it's £300-500 well spent. Once chain skip triggers camshaft correlation codes (P0016/P0017), the full timing kit is mandatory. If the engine has jumped timing causing valve contact, only a full replacement engine makes financial sense.
problems.cta: Get M274 or M264 timing chain replacement quotes for your GLC

problems.group: Mercedes GLC M276 Camshaft Adjuster Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes GLC M276 Camshaft Adjuster Failure - The AMG Cold Start Nightmare
problems.rootCause: The variable valve timing camshaft adjusters (phasers) wear internally, especially the locking mechanisms, causing the adjusters to rattle on cold starts. Extended oil change intervals accelerate wear. Fault codes P001177, P001277, P001685, P001885 typically appear .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace both bank camshaft adjusters, reseal timing covers, new oil and filter. 12-14 hour job.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt engine with revised adjusters
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt M276 engine with factory-revised adjusters, precision balancing, new timing chain and guides, AMG-specific calibration

problems.recommendation: The M276 is a £25,000-£35,000 car at current market values. If the rattle has just started with no metal in the oil or fault codes, the intermediate adjuster replacement is the value play - find a specialist who has done this job before. If the engine has been run with adjuster failure to the point of camshaft pulse wheel damage or code P001177 persists after adjuster replacement, step up to a full rebuilt unit from an AMG specialist. Never use non-OEM adjusters on this engine - they fail within 10,000 miles.
problems.cta: Compare M276 rebuilt engine prices for your GLC 43 AMG

problems.group: Mercedes GLC OM642 Oil Cooler Seal Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes GLC OM642 Oil Cooler Seal Failure - The V6 Diesel Expensive Leak
problems.rootCause: The oil cooler seals (located in the engine "V" between cylinder banks) degrade over time, causing slow oil leaks that drip onto the exhaust system, producing burning smell and eventually low oil pressure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake manifold, fuel injectors, wiring harness to access and replace seals (oil cooler and turbo oil feed). 10-12 hour job.
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles - original seals will fail again

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt engine with Viton upgraded seals
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt OM642 engine with upgraded Viton oil cooler seals, new injector seals, turbo oil feed pipe, full gasket set
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ miles - upgraded seals resolve the design flaw

problems.recommendation: If you catch the leak early (small puddle, occasional smell but no pressure drop), the seal replacement alone is the right move - but use a specialist who fits upgraded Viton seals, not OEM. Many OM642 engines are written off because owners ignore the leak until oil starvation destroys the bottom end. On a GLC 350d, £1,200 well spent at a specialist prevents a £5,000+ replacement.
problems.cta: Get OM642 rebuilt engine quotes for your GLC 350d

problems.group: Mercedes GLC AdBlue Injector & NOx Sensor Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes GLC AdBlue Injector & NOx Sensor Failure - The Emissions Compliance Crisis
problems.rootCause: AdBlue (DEF) crystallises inside the injector nozzle (A0010780108), blocking flow and triggering countdown start inhibitors. NOx sensors fail due to internal heating element degradation, causing permanent emissions warning lights and failed MOT.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injector, ultrasonic clean nozzle, perform SCR system recalibration
repairOptions.longevity: 6-12 months - preventative, not a permanent fix

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace AdBlue injector, flush tank and lines, reset SCR counter with diagnostic tool

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt engine with emissions system overhaul
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt OM654 engine with new AdBlue injector, NOx sensors, EGR cooler, and DPF professionally cleaned
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k miles - emissions components reset to new condition

problems.recommendation: For a GLC still under 60,000 miles, replace the AdBlue injector and NOx sensors individually as they fail - but replace both NOx sensors as a pair (upstream and downstream) to avoid repeat workshop visits. If you're facing a third emissions component failure within 12 months, price up a full rebuilt engine - the labour to repeatedly access the SCR system becomes uneconomical.
problems.cta: Compare OM654 emissions repair quotes for your GLC diesel

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes GLC Class
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing OM654 EGR cooler failure, M274 timing chain rattle, or M276 camshaft adjuster issues on your AMG, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare quotes from trusted UK Mercedes GLC Class specialists. Every rebuilt engine in our network carries upgraded components addressing the original design weakness - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty with nationwide delivery and supply & fit options. Don't pay main dealer premiums. Compare prices from independent Mercedes specialists who know these engines inside out.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes Benz GLC Class Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates (£150-£250/hour) and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes Benz GLC Class and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes Benz GLC Class Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the GLC Class across X253, C253 and X254 generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, years and common failure notes for each.

groups.title: Mercedes Benz GLC Class Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLC Class 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre diesel range forms the backbone of GLC diesel sales in the UK, spanning both the older OM651 (2015-2019) and the newer OM654 (2019-present) engines. The OM651 delivered strong torque and respectable economy, while the OM654 introduced stepped bowl combustion and lower friction for Euro 6d compliance. The OM654's EGR cooler cracking has become the most searched diesel failure pattern for this model.
commonFailurePoints: AdBlue injector clogging; OM651 swirl flap actuator failure
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz GLC Class 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLC Class 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre V6 diesel (OM642) powered the range-topping GLC 350d from 2015 to 2019, offering refined six-cylinder performance and 258hp. While smoother than the four-cylinder alternatives, its complex V6 design created expensive repair challenges, particularly oil leaks from the oil cooler seals located deep in the engine's 'V'. The OM642 was discontinued in the GLC range after the 2019 facelift as Mercedes shifted to four-cylinder electrified diesels.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz GLC Class 3.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Mercedes Benz GLC Class Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLC Class 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre petrol range covers the majority of non-AMG GLC petrol sales. The M274 (2015-2019) powered earlier GLC 200, 250 and 300 variants, while the M264 (2019-present) introduced EQ Boost mild hybrid technology with a 48V belt-driven starter-generator. The M254 (2022-present) appears in the latest X254 generation with further refinement. Timing chain tensioner wear on the M274 is the most frequently searched petrol failure for this model.
commonFailurePoints: M274 timing chain tensioner wear (cold start rattle); M264 48V belt-driven starter-generator failure; M254 integrated exhaust manifold turbo wear
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz GLC Class 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLC Class 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre V6 biturbo (M276) powered the GLC 43 AMG from 2016 to 2021, delivering 367-390hp through a twin-turbocharged V6 configuration. This engine offered AMG performance as the entry point below the V8 flagship, with strong mid-range punch and V6 refinement. The M276 is known for camshaft adjuster wear, which manifests as a pronounced rattle on cold starts and can trigger multiple camshaft correlation fault codes.
commonFailurePoints: Camshaft adjuster (phaser) wear causing cold start rattle; camshaft solenoid seal oil leaks; high-pressure fuel pump failure
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz GLC Class 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLC Class 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.0-litre V8 biturbo (M177) powered the flagship GLC 63 and 63 S AMG from 2017 to 2022. This hand-built engine, derived from the AMG GT, produces 476-510hp through a hot-vee design with twin-scroll turbos positioned inside the cylinder vee for improved response. The M177 represents the ultimate GLC performance option, with correspondingly high replacement costs and specialist-only rebuild requirements.
commonFailurePoints: Cylinder bore scoring on higher-mileage examples; oil consumption from piston ring wear; turbocharger failure due to oil starvation
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz GLC Class 4.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Mercedes Benz GLC Class Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLC Class 2.0L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The GLC 350e (2016-2022) combined the M274 petrol engine with an electric motor and 13.5kWh battery, offering approximately 30 miles of electric range. The second-generation GLC 300e (2023-present) uses the M254 engine with an upgraded plug-in hybrid system delivering 313hp combined. These complex drivetrains require specialist knowledge for repair, and hybrid component failures often drive replacement decisions. Not sure which engine size your Mercedes Benz GLC Class needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Mercedes Benz GLC Class specialists.
commonFailurePoints: Hybrid battery degradation; electric motor bearing failure in drive unit; charging system inverter faults
items.cta: Enquire about Mercedes Benz GLC Class 2.0L hybrid engine availability

fuelTypes.h2: Mercedes Benz GLC Class Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Mercedes Benz GLC Class engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from the efficient GLC 220d to the ferocious GLC 63 AMG.

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLC Class Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel powered the majority of GLC sales across the X253 (2015-2022) and X254 (2022-present) generations, with the OM651 and OM654 four-cylinders dominating replacement enquiries - particularly the OM654's EGR cooler failures.
items.descriptor: Diesel powered the majority of GLC sales across the X253 (2015-2022) and X254 (2022-present) generations, with the OM651 and OM654 four-cylinders dominating replacement enquiries - particularly the OM654's EGR cooler failures.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mercedes Benz GLC Class Diesel Engine Families
families: OM651 - 2.1L four-cylinder (2015-2019 GLC 220d, 250d), known for swirl flap and injector issues
OM654 - 2.0L four-cylinder (2019-present GLC 220d, 300d), EGR cooler cracking is the most searched failure
OM642 - 3.0L V6 (2015-2019 GLC 350d), oil cooler seal leaks in the engine 'V' drive replacement decisions
foundIn: X253 GLC Class SUV / C253 Coupe (2015-2022) - OM651, OM654, OM642
X254 GLC Class SUV / C254 Coupe (2022-present) - OM654 only
knownFor: Strong motorway economy - 50+ mpg on OM654-equipped 220d models
OM654 EGR cooler cracking between 40k-80k miles - the dominant replacement driver
OM651 timing chain stretch and swirl flap actuator failure on higher-mileage units
OM642 V6 oil leaks that often exceed vehicle value to repair properly

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLC Class Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol GLC models range from the economical 2.0-litre four-cylinder M274 and M264 units to the M276 V6 in the GLC 43 and the hand-built M177 V8 in the flagship GLC 63 - each with distinct replacement cost tiers.
items.descriptor: Petrol GLC models range from the economical 2.0-litre four-cylinder M274 and M264 units to the M276 V6 in the GLC 43 and the hand-built M177 V8 in the flagship GLC 63 - each with distinct replacement cost tiers.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mercedes Benz GLC Class Petrol Engine Families
families: M274 - 2.0L four-cylinder (2015-2019 GLC 200, 250, 300), timing chain tensioner wear is the #1 failure
M264 - 2.0L mild hybrid (2019-present GLC 200, 300), 48V belt-driven starter-generator (BSG) adds complexity
M276 - 3.0L V6 biturbo (2016-2021 GLC 43 AMG), camshaft adjuster failure on cold starts
M177 - 4.0L V8 biturbo (2017-2022 GLC 63, 63 S), cylinder bore scoring at higher mileages
foundIn: X253 GLC Class SUV / C253 Coupe (2015-2022) - M274, M276, M177
X254 GLC Class SUV / C254 Coupe (2022-present) - M264, M254
knownFor: M274 timing chain rattle on cold start - catch it early or risk engine replacement
M264 EQ Boost mild hybrid system - specialist knowledge required for 48V component work
M276 camshaft adjuster wear - a £2k-£4k repair on a £25k-£35k vehicle
M177 hand-built V8 - ultra-premium replacement costs (£7k-£14k supply only)

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLC Class Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV) Engines
items.description: The GLC 350e (2016-2022) and second-generation GLC 300e/400e (2023-present) combine petrol engines with electric motors and batteries - complex systems requiring specialist PHEV knowledge for engine replacement.
items.descriptor: The GLC 350e (2016-2022) and second-generation GLC 300e/400e (2023-present) combine petrol engines with electric motors and batteries - complex systems requiring specialist PHEV knowledge for engine replacement.
items.familiesLabel: Mercedes Benz GLC Class PHEV Engine Codes
families: M274 Hybrid - 2.0L petrol plus electric motor (GLC 350e, 2016-2022)
M254 PHEV - 2.0L petrol plus upgraded hybrid system (GLC 300e, 400e, 2023-present)
Electric motor paired with M274 or M254 petrol engine for combined 313-320hp
13.5kWh battery (first gen) offers approximately 30 miles electric-only range
Regenerative braking recovers energy to charge the battery during deceleration
importantNotes: Hybrid battery health inspection is essential before any engine replacement
Specialist PHEV-certified mechanics required - not all general engine shops qualify
Replacement availability is more limited than standard diesel or petrol units
Engine removal requires high-voltage system isolation - additional labour time

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLC Class Mild Hybrid (MHEV) Engines
items.description: The M264 and M254 petrol engines in facelift X253 and current X254 GLC models feature 48V EQ Boost mild hybrid technology - adding complexity to engine replacement but improving throttle response and efficiency.
items.descriptor: The M264 and M254 petrol engines in facelift X253 and current X254 GLC models feature 48V EQ Boost mild hybrid technology - adding complexity to engine replacement but improving throttle response and efficiency.
items.familiesLabel: Mercedes Benz GLC Class MHEV Engine Codes
families: M264 - 2.0L petrol with 48V belt-driven starter-generator (BSG) - GLC 200, 300 from 2019
M254 - 2.0L petrol with integrated starter-generator (ISG) - current generation GLC
48V belt-driven starter-generator (M264) or integrated unit (M254) replaces traditional alternator
Provides up to 20hp electric boost during acceleration
Recovers braking energy to charge the 48V lithium-ion battery
importantNotes: Failed 48V BSG (A2641500060) is a known failure on M264 - can drive replacement if internal engine damage occurs
MHEV components require specialist diagnostics and coding after engine fitment
48V system must be isolated before any engine work - safety protocols add to labour time
Not all independent Mercedes specialists are equipped for MHEV engine swaps

engineYears.h2: Mercedes Benz GLC Class Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers all major engine changes across the GLC Class production run (2015 to present), helping owners identify their exact powerplant and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The GLC replaced the GLK in 2015, debuting on Mercedes' MRA platform with OM651 diesel and M274 petrol four-cylinders.
keyChanges: New X253 (SUV) and C253 (Coupe) body styles launched
9G-Tronic automatic transmission introduced across all variants
4MATIC all-wheel drive optional or standard depending on variant
Early build M274 engines subject to timing chain tensioner recall
knownFor: Strong residual values and premium SUV refinement
M274 timing chain tensioner wear - cold start rattle from 60k miles
OM651 swirl flap actuator failures and injector carbon build-up
OM642 oil cooler seal leaks in the V6 engine 'V' - labour-intensive repair
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2019 Mercedes GLC Class replacement engines
years.ticker: GLC 220d engine replacement • OM651 swirl flap failure • M274 timing chain rattle • GLC 350d oil leak repair

years.description: The GLC 350e plug-in hybrid joined the range, combining the M274 petrol engine with an electric motor and 13.5kWh battery.
keyChanges: GLC 350e PHEV launched with up to 30 miles electric range
Hybrid system shared with C-Class 350e
Battery pack positioned under boot floor (reducing cargo space)
Charging port integrated into rear bumper
knownFor: Complex PHEV system requires specialist knowledge for engine replacement
Hybrid battery degradation common after 80k miles
High-voltage system must be isolated before any engine work
Limited supply of experienced PHEV engine specialists in UK
years.cta: Compare PHEV engine prices for your GLC 350e
years.ticker: GLC 350e engine replacement • GLC PHEV engine cost • M274 hybrid engine rebuild

years.description: The 2019 facelift brought the OM654 2.0-litre diesel and M264 mild-hybrid petrol engines - along with the EGR cooler failure that now dominates GLC diesel replacement enquiries .
keyChanges: OM651 2.1L diesel replaced by OM654 2.0L diesel (Euro 6d compliant)
M274 replaced by M264 with EQ Boost 48V mild hybrid system (BSG)
10.25-inch infotainment screen and digital instrument cluster added
GLC 200 petrol became base model (2WD instead of 4MATIC)
knownFor: OM654 EGR cooler cracking (A6540940101) - dominant replacement driver between 40k-80k miles
M264 48V belt-driven starter-generator (BSG) failures - A2641500060
OM654 AdBlue injector nozzle clogging requiring SCR recalibration
M276 camshaft adjuster wear on AMG models
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019-2022 facelift GLC Class engines
years.ticker: GLC 220d OM654 engine replacement • OM654 EGR cooler failure • M264 BSG replacement • GLC 43 AMG engine cost

years.description: The GLC 43 AMG brought a 3.0-litre V6 biturbo to the range, offering 367-390hp as the performance entry point below the V8 GLC 63.
keyChanges: GLC 43 AMG launched 2016 (367hp), upgraded 2019 facelift (390hp)
9G-Tronic with AMG-specific software and paddle shifters
Standard 4MATIC+ variable all-wheel drive
AMG Ride Control sports suspension with adjustable damping
knownFor: Camshaft adjuster (phaser) wear - cold start rattle, codes P001177/P001277
Oil leaks from camshaft solenoid seals
High-pressure fuel pump failures on tuned or high-mileage examples
Specialist rebuild required - not all engine shops work on M276
years.cta: Compare M276 V6 prices for your GLC 43 AMG
years.ticker: GLC 43 AMG engine replacement • M276 camshaft adjuster repair • GLC 43 AMG rebuilt engine price

years.description: The hand-built M177 V8 biturbo powered the range-topping GLC 63 and 63 S AMG - 476-510hp from a hot-vee design shared with the AMG GT.
keyChanges: GLC 63 launched 2017 (476hp), GLC 63 S 2018 (510hp)
AMG Speedshift MCT 9-speed transmission (wet clutch instead of torque converter)
Standard 4MATIC+ with drift mode on S models
Active engine mounts and AMG cylinder management
knownFor: Cylinder bore scoring on higher-mileage examples (60k+ miles)
Oil consumption from piston ring wear - common on early builds
Turbocharger failure due to oil starvation - very high replacement cost
Ultra-premium pricing - rebuilt units £7k-£14k supply only
years.cta: Enquire about GLC 63 AMG M177 engine availability

years.description: The all-new X254 GLC launched for 2022, bringing full electrification across the range - all engines now feature mild hybrid or plug-in hybrid systems.
keyChanges: New platform with longer wheelbase (+2 inches) and increased boot space
Full electrification: every engine has 48V mild hybrid or PHEV system
M254 2.0L petrol replaces M264 with integrated starter-generator (ISG)
GLC 300e PHEV offers 54 miles electric range (up from 30 miles on previous gen)
knownFor: All engines electrified - 48V ISG on M254 replaces belt-driven BSG from M264
OM654 continues - EGR cooler cracking still a concern on post-2022 units
GLC 300e offers class-leading electric range for premium PHEV SUVs
New 2.0L AMG models replace V6/V8 - GLC 43 now uses electric turbo four-cylinder
years.cta: Get quotes for 2022-present X254 GLC Class engines

years.description: The second-generation GLC 43 and 63 AMG swapped V6/V8 engines for electrified 2.0L four-cylinders with electric turbocharging.
keyChanges: GLC 43 AMG replaces M276 V6 with M139l 2.0L four-cylinder + 48V mild hybrid
GLC 63 S E Performance adds 201hp electric motor on rear axle - 671hp total
Both AMG models include rear-wheel steering as standard
Hand-built Affalterbach engines continue despite displacement reduction
mainEngines: M139l PHEV - 2.0L plug-in hybrid (GLC 63 S E Performance) - 671hp combined
knownFor: Electric turbocharger eliminates lag - first in GLC AMG
GLC 63 S E Performance is most powerful GLC ever built
PHEV battery on 63 S adds weight but enables electric-only driving
Too new for widespread failure data - reliability unproven as of 2026

faq.h2: Mercedes Benz GLC Class Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mercedes GLC engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt GLC engine typically costs £2,400-£4,800 for the common 220d diesel, £2,200-£4,000 for the 300 petrol, and £7,000-£14,000 for the 63 AMG V8. Supply only - fitting adds £600-£1,200.
keyPoints: GLC 220d (OM654): rebuilt unit £2,400-£4,200; used engine from £1,500
GLC 300 (M264): rebuilt unit £2,500-£4,500; used engine from £1,800
GLC 43 AMG (M276): rebuilt unit £4,500-£8,000; specialist fit only
GLC 63 AMG (M177): rebuilt unit £7,000-£14,000; V8 specialist required
Price depends on engine code, year, mileage, and supplier warranty length
items.cta: Compare Mercedes GLC engine replacement quotes from UK specialists

items.question: Is the Mercedes GLC 220d engine reliable? What are the common problems?
items.answer: The 220d's OM654 engine is fundamentally strong, but EGR cooler cracking between 40k-80k miles is the dominant failure - often requiring full engine replacement if coolant loss causes overheating.
keyPoints: OM654 EGR cooler (part A6540940101) fractures from thermal stress - most searched GLC diesel fault
AdBlue injector nozzle clogging (A0010780108) triggers countdown warnings
NOx sensor failure causes permanent emissions lights and failed MOT
OM651 in pre-2019 220ds suffers swirl flap actuator failure and timing chain stretch
Well-maintained OM654 diesels regularly exceed 200,000 miles despite these issues
items.cta: Get quotes for GLC 220d OM654 replacement engines

items.question: Which Mercedes GLC engine is most reliable - petrol or diesel?
items.answer: The OM654 diesel (2019-onwards) delivers the best long-term reliability at high mileage. For petrol buyers, the M264 has more complex 48V hybrid components to fail, while the older M274 suffers timing chain tensioner wear.
keyPoints: GLC 250d with OM651 is considered "virtually indestructible" when maintained
GLC 300 with M264 offers good reliability but BSG (48V starter-generator) can fail
GLC 220d OM654 has one known weak point (EGR cooler) but otherwise very robust
M274 petrol engines pre-2017 have higher timing chain failure rates
Second-gen X254 (2022+) mild hybrids too new for long-term reliability data
items.cta: Find a reconditioned GLC engine with 12-month warranty

items.question: What are the signs of timing chain failure on a Mercedes GLC?
items.answer: A 1-3 second rattling noise on cold start is the classic warning - most common on M274 petrol engines (GLC 200/250/300, 2015-2019). Ignoring it risks engine seizure and £5,000+ repair bills.
keyPoints: Cold start rattle that disappears after oil pressure builds - earliest warning sign
Check engine light with cam/crank correlation codes (P0016/P0017)
Metallic swarf in engine oil indicates chain guides wearing away
Rough idle or misfire means timing has already drifted
Preventative tensioner replacement costs £300-500 - full chain kit £1,200-1,700
items.cta: Compare M274 timing chain repair costs for your GLC

items.question: How long do Mercedes GLC engines last?
items.answer: Properly maintained GLC diesel engines routinely reach 250,000-300,000 miles (400,000-500,000 km) before major work. Petrol engines typically need significant attention between 120,000-180,000 miles .
keyPoints: OM651 and OM654 diesels: 250,000+ miles with regular servicing
M274/M264 petrols: 150,000-200,000 miles before major repairs likely
OM654 EGR cooler typically fails once - after replacement, engine continues strongly
Service interval is 10,000 miles or 12 months - use Mercedes-spec oil only
AMG V8 engines (M276/M177) require more frequent maintenance - 120,000-150,000 miles typical
items.cta: Get quotes for a high-mileage GLC engine replacement

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Mercedes GLC?
items.answer: Yes, if the GLC is otherwise in good condition with no rust or major electrical faults. A rebuilt OM654 or M264 engine costs £2,500-£4,500 - far less than replacing a £18,000-£35,000 vehicle.
keyPoints: GLC values remain strong - X253 (2015-2022) still worth £15,000-£30,000+
Rebuilt engine with upgraded components (EGR cooler, timing chain tensioner) fixes original weak points
Supply & fit typically £3,000-£6,000 depending on engine code
New engine includes 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - peace of mind
Only consider scrapping if body corrosion, transmission failure, or structural damage present
items.cta: Compare GLC engine rebuild vs replacement costs

items.question: What engine oil does my Mercedes GLC need and how often?
items.answer: Mercedes-Benz specifies 229.51 or 229.52 low-SAPS oil (5W-30 or 0W-20 depending on year). Change every 10,000 miles or 12 months - stretched intervals cause timing chain wear and turbo failure .
keyPoints: OM654 diesel: MB 229.52 0W-20 or 5W-30 - low-ash formula protects DPF
M274/M264 petrol: MB 229.5 5W-30 or 0W-40 - high-spec synthetic only
Never use non-approved oil - destroys timing chain tensioners and hydraulic lifters
OM642 V6 diesel requires 8.5 litres of 5W-30 low-SAPS oil
Late oil changes are the #1 cause of preventable engine failure on all GLC engines
items.cta: Get quotes for GLC engine replacement from MB specialists

items.question: What causes AdBlue failure on Mercedes GLC diesels?
items.answer: AdBlue crystallises inside the injector nozzle (A0010780108), blocking flow and triggering countdown starters - OM654 engines (2019+) are most affected. NOx sensor failure is the second most common emissions fault.
keyPoints: Crystallisation blocks the injector - ultrasonic cleaning helps but replacement often needed
Countdown starts at 16 starts remaining - ignore and car won't restart
NOx sensors fail due to internal heating element degradation
Repair cost: injector £280-450, sensor replacement £350-600 each (replace both)
Using poor-quality AdBlue or letting tank run dry accelerates injector failure
items.cta: Compare OM654 AdBlue system repair prices

items.question: Are second-generation X254 GLC engines (2022+) reliable?
items.answer: Too early for definitive long-term data, but early owner feedback suggests OM654M diesels and M254 mild hybrids are proving more reliable than first-gen facelift engines. No major systematic failures reported.
keyPoints: OM654M diesel includes revised EGR cooler - fewer cracking reports so far
Integrated starter-generator (ISG) replaces problematic belt-driven BSG from M264
GLC 300e PHEV offers 80-mile electric range - battery health unknown long-term
GLC 43 now uses M139l four-cylinder - reliability unproven, early AMG feedback mixed
First major repairs expected after 100,000-150,000km - watch this space
items.cta: Enquire about X254 GLC hybrid and mild hybrid engine availability

items.question: Should I buy a GLC 300e plug-in hybrid or stick with diesel?
items.answer: Choose the 300e PHEV if you have home charging and mostly do sub-50 mile daily trips - 5% BIK tax saves company car drivers £3,000 annually over the 220d. Choose diesel for high-mileage motorway use where battery range won't be utilised .
keyPoints: GLC 300e offers 55-80 miles electric range - covers most daily commutes
5% Benefit-in-Kind (BIK) tax vs 37% for diesel - huge company car saving
Petrol PHEV cheaper to buy than diesel 300de - £3,000/year lower tax
Real-world economy on long journeys: diesel 45-50mpg, PHEV 35-40mpg (battery depleted)
Diesel better for towing - PHEV's extra battery weight reduces payload
items.cta: Compare GLC 300e PHEV and 220d diesel replacement options
